rulings.Case Title: PAUL R BARNES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, November 12, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant American Sterilizer Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Barnes was exposed to asbestos-containing materials or products from an American Sterilizer autoclave. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Barnes¿ deposition and Mr. Cohen¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CASEY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, November 12, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion for summary adjudication as to defendant Hamilton Materials¿ affirmative defenses is granted as to affirmative defenses numbers 9, 10, 12, 13, 23, 24 and 25 because defendant did not dispute them and is granted as to affirmative defenses numbers 2, 7, 11, 18, 19 and 20 because plaintiffs sustained their burden of production and defendant failed to present evidence creating a triable issue as to any of these affirmative defenses. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CASEY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, November 12, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication: hearing required. It is possible, though far from certain, that a tentative ruling may be available tomorrow November 11. If you wish to receive it, please send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating the email address(es) that the tentative ruling should be sent. Late Tentative Ruling: Defendant Golden Gate Drywall¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production (by the declaration of Joel Cohen and plaintiffs¿ factually-devoid discovery responses) and plaintiffs did not present admissible evidence that defendant knew or should have known that it exposed Mr. Casey to asbestos. The objection to Dr. Richard Cohen¿s declaration is sustained. = (220/HEK)
